perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and with the written consent (including via email, which such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the Company or as referenced on the attached Schedule A of this Agreement any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and without the need of the written consent of the Company or as referenced on the attached Schedule A of this Agreement any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement of written consent from the Company for consultants and potential financing sources to be considered 'Representatives,' this change contradicts Section 3, which requires the Company's written consent for disclosing Transaction Information to any person. This creates uncertainty as to whether GTCR needs Vocus's consent to share information with its consultants and financing sources. This also removes obligations of the Representatives as they are defined in Section 2 where it states 'You shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of your Representatives.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as set forth in Sections 2 and 4 of this Agreement, you agree that without the prior written consent of Vocus, neither you nor any of your Representatives shall disclose to any person the existence of this Agreement, the fact that the Evaluation Material exists or has been made available to you or any of your Representatives, the fact that you have any interest in pursuing a Transaction involving the Company, the fact that discussions or negotiations concerning a Transaction are or may be taking place, or have taken place between you and the Company (and/or any of the Company’s Representatives), or any of the terms, conditions or other matters discussed between you and the Company (and/or any of the Company’s Representatives) with respect thereto (the foregoing such information described in this sentence being hereafter referred to collectively as “ Transaction Information ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text requires written consent from the Company for consultants and potential financing sources to be considered 'Representatives.' The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that 'neither you nor any of your Representatives shall disclose to any person' Transaction Information without prior written consent. By removing the consent requirement in the definition of 'Representative,' the changed text now contradicts the contradicted text. Under the changed text, consultants and financing sources are automatically 'Representatives' without needing consent, but the contradicted text still requires consent before they can be given information. Therefore, there is a direct and clear contradiction as GTCR no longer needs consent to share information with its consultants and financing sources.

perturbation.original_text: Evaluation Material . As used in this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” means all information concerning the Company (whether furnished on or after the date hereof, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise, whether or not marked as being confidential, and irrespective of the form of communication, including oral as well as written and electronic communications) that is furnished to you or to your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Evaluation Material . As used in this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” means only written information concerning the Company (whether furnished on or after the date hereof, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise, whether or not marked as being confidential) that is furnished to you or to your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting 'Evaluation Material' to only *written* information, this modification contradicts Section 2, which states that Evaluation Material encompasses all forms of communication, including oral. This creates ambiguity about whether oral disclosures are subject to the NDA's confidentiality requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Evaluation Material' as all information, including oral and written. The changed text restricts the definition to only *written* information. Section 2, the contradicted text, refers to 'Evaluation Material' without specifying its form (written or oral). Because the changed text now excludes oral information from the definition of 'Evaluation Material', it contradicts Section 2's implication that all forms of information shared are subject to the confidentiality requirements. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” also includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations and other documents prepared by you or your Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information that the Company or the Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 Company’s Representatives furnish to you or your Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” also includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations and other documents prepared by you or your Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, only if approved by the Company, in whole or in part, the information that the Company or the Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 Company’s Representatives furnish to you or your Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the requirement for company approval before notes, analyses, etc., become 'Evaluation Material', this contradicts section 5 regarding the 'Return of Evaluation Material'. The original language forces the return of *all* Evaluation Material, but this addition creates uncertainty as to which notes and analyses must be returned, making enforcement inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon the request of the Company for any reason, you shall (and will direct your Representatives to) promptly, and in any event no later than 10 days after receipt of the request, deliver to the Company, or at your option, destroy, all Evaluation Material (including all copies, extracts and other reproductions thereof, whether in paper, electronic or other form or media) furnished to you or your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company pursuant to this Agreement; provided, however, that you and your Representatives shall be entitled to retain one complete copy, in electronic archival storage form, of all Evaluation Material in accordance with document retention laws or regulations applicable to you and to such other persons, as the case may be, but only to the extent that appropriate personnel whose primary function within your organization and within the organizations of such other persons, as the case may be, is information technology or compliance in nature will have unrestricted access to such retained information; and provided, further, however, that your legal counsel will be entitled to retain one complete copy of Evaluation Material in paper format as may be necessary to document your consideration of a Transaction for the purpose of establishing compliance with any applicable Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 4 laws or regulations and for defending and maintaining any litigation (or any arbitral or administrative case or proceeding) relating to this Agreement or the Evaluation Material; provided, that all such information shall continue to be kept confidential and shall be stored only in counsel’s record archives to which access is not made generally available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a requirement for company approval before certain documents are considered 'Evaluation Material.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates the return or destruction of *all* Evaluation Material. The added approval requirement creates uncertainty and ambiguity about which documents must be returned, potentially leading to disputes and making the original return obligation unenforceable. The original text requires all evaluation materials to be returned but the Changed text provides an escape hatch, hence a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that for a period of eighteen (18) months from and after the date hereof (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither you nor any of your affiliates, directly or indirectly, shall:
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that for a period of eighteen (18) months from and after the date hereof (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither you nor any of your affiliates, directly or indirectly, shall, unless expressly permitted by the GTCR Investment Committee:
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'unless expressly permitted by the GTCR Investment Committee,' to the standstill clause, a condition is introduced that allows GTCR to bypass the restrictions outlined in Section 8. This weakens the protection Vocus seeks and creates a conflict with the original, stricter terms. For example it now contradicts 8(d) where GTCR can simply make public announcement, so long as permitted by their GTCR Investment Committee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: unless expressly invited to do so by the Board of Directors of Vocus (or any duly constituted committee thereof comprised wholly of independent directors of Vocus), make any public announcement with respect to, or submit to the Company or any of its affiliates, Representatives or any other person, any proposal, expression of interest, term sheet, memorandum of understanding, letter of intent, inquiry or offer (with or without conditions) providing for, in a single transaction or in any series of related transactions, any merger, consolidation, acquisition, business combination, share exchange, recapitalization, reorganization, divestiture, spin-off, split-off, cash or property distribution or any other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any of the Company’s securities, assets or businesses;
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither the party nor its affiliates can make any public announcement, while the changed text adds an exception: unless expressly permitted by the GTCR Investment Committee. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that public announcements can only be made if expressly invited by the Board of Directors of Vocus. The addition weakens the protection Vocus seeks, by giving GTCR the ability to make public announcement, so long as permitted by their GTCR Investment Committee, directly contradicting the requirement in the original contradicted text of being expressly invited by the Board of Directors of Vocus. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing sentence (and subject to the provisions of Section 8 of this Agreement), each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that the other party hereto may disclose information about itself (if disclosed by you or your Representatives, to the extent not containing any Transaction Information or Evaluation Material, and if disclosed by the Company or its Representatives, to the extent not containing any Transaction Information) to, and enter into negotiations with, other persons or entities at any time without any obligation to notify the other party hereto of such disclosure or negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing sentence (and subject to the provisions of Section 8 of this Agreement), each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that the other party hereto may disclose information about itself (if disclosed by you or your Representatives, without restriction, and if disclosed by the Company or its Representatives, to the extent not containing any Transaction Information) to, and enter into negotiations with, other persons or entities at any time without any obligation to notify the other party hereto of such disclosure or negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'to the extent not containing any Transaction Information or Evaluation Material' clause, GTCR can now disclose Evaluation Material. This is a direct contradiction to Section 2 which forbids to disclose any Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the disclosure of 'Transaction Information or Evaluation Material', whereas the changed text removes the restriction on disclosing 'Evaluation Material' by GTCR. The contradicted text in Section 2 explicitly prohibits the disclosure of 'Evaluation Material'. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts Section 2, as GTCR can now disclose 'Evaluation Material' when it was previously forbidden.

perturbation.original_text: The parties to this Agreement hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of the state courts of the State of Delaware or of the United States District Court for the District of Delaware for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by United States registered mail, postage prepaid, to their address set forth herein shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against it in any such court.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties to this Agreement hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of the state courts of the State of Delaware or of the United States District Court for the District of Delaware for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement EXCEPT FOR GTCR, GTCR may choose jurisdiction to be Illinois, and the transactions contemplated hereby, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by United States registered mail, postage prepaid, to their address set forth herein shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against it in any such court.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'EXCEPT FOR GTCR, GTCR may choose jurisdiction to be Illinois,' GTCR can now choose to be sued in another state court outside of Delaware. This is now contradicting to what the paragraph is stating which that the parties have 'irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of the state courts of the State of Delaware'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties to this Agreement hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of the state courts of the State of Delaware or of the United States District Court for the District of Delaware for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by United States registered mail, postage prepaid, to their address set forth herein shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against it in any such court.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all parties irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of Delaware courts. The changed text introduces an exception for GTCR, allowing them to choose Illinois jurisdiction. This directly contradicts the 'irrevocably and unconditionally' clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, as GTCR is no longer bound by the Delaware jurisdiction under certain circumstances. The changed clause narrows the original obligation.
